entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop studied in Mali for market participation and technology adoption.

entities.name: MALI
entities.description: The country where the study on sorghum household farms was conducted.

entities.name: TRIPLE HURDLE MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model integrating decisions on adoption, market participation, and marketed quantity to control for selection bias and endogeneity.

entities.name: MARKET PARTICIPATION
entities.description: The decision and act of engaging in the sale of agricultural produce in the market.

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL TECHNOLOGY ADOPTION
entities.description: The process of adopting new agricultural technologies, such as improved crop varieties.

entities.name: IMPROVED SORGHUM VARIETIES
entities.description: Enhanced sorghum varieties developed through breeding to improve yield or other traits.

entities.name: MARKETED QUANTITY
entities.description: The volume of sorghum produce that is actually sold in the market.

entities.name: SELECTION BIAS
entities.description: A statistical bias that occurs when the sample is not representative of the population, controlled by the triple-hurdle model.

entities.name: ENDOGENEITY
entities.description: A correlation between an explanatory variable and the error term, controlled by the triple-hurdle model in the context of adoption and participation.

entities.name: SORGHUM BREEDING
entities.description: The scientific process of developing improved sorghum varieties.

entities.name: MARKET OPPORTUNITY
entities.description: The availability of favorable conditions for selling sorghum in the market.

entities.name: MARKET DEMAND
entities.description: The consumer demand for sorghum, which should influence breeding programs.

entities.name: MARKET ACCESS
entities.description: The ability of smallholder farmers to reach and participate in markets.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMERS
entities.description: The farmers managing sorghum household farms in Mali.
